title: Separate source-of-truth (.json) from derived artifacts (.sqlite) in git
context: MCP server constantly rewrites .hunch/hunch.sqlite{,-wal,-shm} indices. These writes leave the working tree perpetually dirty, blocking branch switches, pulls, and rebases. The SQLite index is regenerable from committed .hunch/*.json—it shouldn't be versioned. Touches invariant(s): con_8460b6770f (Config/provider writers must merge idempotently into existing user files and refuse to clobber an unparseable file).
decision: Mark .hunch/*.sqlite* as gitignored (derived, regenerable). Keep .hunch/*.json tracked (source of truth). Wire ensureGitignore() into `init` and `index` commands—idempotent, merge-safe, marked block to avoid clobbering user's .gitignore.
consequences: Working tree stays clean after MCP writes; no more git operation blocks
Existing repos with committed .sqlite need one-time manual untrack: `git rm --cached .hunch/*.sqlite*`
New setups inherit clean state automatically (wired into init before first index write)
.gitignore becomes a managed artifact; marked block allows re-runs without rewrites
alternatives_rejected: Store SQLite outside .hunch/ (breaks co-location design, makes path discovery harder)
Disable index writes (kills query performance, not viable)
Accept perpetually dirty working tree (unacceptable UX—blocks rebases)
Store in .git/hunch/ or temp dir (less discoverable, harder to inspect)
Gitignore only at init time (doesn't help repos adding hunch later)
